reason: The document migration REST API (POST /v2/organizations/documents/migrate and POST /v2/organizations/documents/migrate/status) is a document-level backend administration API, not a Comments, Notifications, or CRDT SDK feature. Although the migration preserves comment, notification, and CRDT data as an implementation detail, no developer-facing API in the Comments, Notifications, or CRDT surface areas was added, changed, renamed, or removed. The persistence batch-size reduction (500→100) is an internal server-side bug fix with no observable API or behavior change. No skill library rule needs to be created or updated.

itemsEvaluated.releaseNoteText: Added two endpoints for asynchronous document migration. POST /v2/organizations/documents/migrate initiates a migration that re-keys a document to a new ID while preserving all associated data (comments, reactions, recorder, notifications, and related subcollections). POST /v2/organizations/documents/migrate/status polls progress using the migrationId returned by the initiate call.
itemsEvaluated.reason: Endpoints reside under /v2/organizations/documents/, not under /v2/notifications/, /v2/commentannotations/, or /v2/crdt/. This is a document-lifecycle admin API. All three skill sets are scoped to developer-facing Comments, Notifications, and CRDT SDK features. Document migration endpoints do not belong to any of those skill sets and no existing skill rule references document re-keying.

itemsEvaluated.releaseNoteText: Fixed 'Transaction too big' errors during document migration by reducing persistence batch size from 500 to 100 in subcollection copy, notification copy, and user doc config operations. Migrations involving documents with large notification or comment payloads will no longer fail.
itemsEvaluated.reason: Pure internal server-side fix. No developer API or documented behavior changed. Bug fixes do not qualify as skill deltas unless they alter documented API or observable behavior.
